checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet comparison Q1 vs Q2 2012

evidence.quote: Goodwill=44178184.64 (2010-12-31) as restated in the FY2012 10-K, versus goodwill levels of ~108-109M reported in 2011 interim 10-Qs for similar periods

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K income statement and cash flow statement FY2012
10-K income statement and cash flow statement FY2011

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit margin fell from ~83.5% (Q4 2012: GrossProfit=56143234.70 / SalesRevenueNet=67250508.38) to ~77.3% (Q1 2013: GrossProfit=46553426.85 / SalesRevenueNet=60178805.44)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-21490029.46 (Q1 2011) versus NetIncomeLoss=4780497.60 (Q4 2010) and NetIncomeLoss=7212764.84 (Q1 2012) — a large negative swing followed by recovery, indicating earnings volatility rather than smoothing

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Liabilities=233683136.20 (2011-12-31) declining to 63137503.67 (2012-12-31), a ~73% drop, while SalesRevenueNet declined only modestly from 282196423.01 (2011) to 277513768.34 (2012)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2012-05-01, shortly after the original 10-K filed 2012-02-29
Multiple SEC comment-letter correspondence cycles: UPLOAD 2011-07-12, CORRESP 2011-08-09, UPLOAD 2011-08-25, CORRESP 2011-09-16, UPLOAD 2011-09-29, CORRESP 2013-07-24

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data points spanning fiscal years 2008 through Q1 2013 are provided across Revenue, AR, Inventory, Goodwill, Liabilities, Cash Flow and Net Income concepts

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature/aggressive revenue recognition or extended customer credit terms: receivables grew (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=79731784.96 (2011-12-31) to 90229191.04 (2012-12-31), +13.2%) even as revenue declined (SalesRevenueNet=282196423.01 (2011) to 277513768.34 (2012), -1.7%), while operating cash flow collapsed (NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=6726551.50 (2012) vs NetIncomeLoss=30790510.35 (2012)) and the doubtful-accounts allowance nearly doubled (AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=5628069.50 (2011-12-31) to 9717063.68 (2012-12-31)), consistent with revenue booked ahead of collectability and later reserve catch-up.

A large, apparently undisclosed-in-data divestiture/deconsolidation occurred between Q1 2012 and Q2 2012 (Goodwill fell from 108308523.94 (2012-03-31) to 43885856.37 (2012-06-30); IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill fell from 21848986.97 to 4378321.13; Liabilities fell from 221604036.10 (2012-03-31) to 119514841.09 (2012-06-30); SecuredLongTermDebt fell from 111015711.81 to 24010535.42). A gain associated with this transaction may have inflated FY2012 NetIncomeLoss (30790510.35) without a corresponding increase in operating cash flow, masking weaker core operating cash generation.

Retroactive restatement/reclassification of prior-period goodwill and intangibles: the FY2012 10-K reports 2010-12-31 and 2011-12-31 goodwill at ~44M (44178184.64 and 43875651.89 respectively), materially lower than the ~108-109M levels reported for adjacent 2011 interim periods in contemporaneously filed 10-Qs, implying original purchase-accounting or asset carrying values were later revised downward, consistent with possible prior-period overstatement of intangible assets.

top_signals: FY2012 NetIncomeLoss (30790510.35) far exceeds operating cash flow (6726551.50), a ~4.6x divergence
Accounts receivable grew +13.2% (79731784.96 to 90229191.04) while revenue declined -1.7% (282196423.01 to 277513768.34), with allowance for doubtful accounts nearly doubling

Major, unexplained discontinuities in Goodwill, Intangibles, Liabilities and Secured Debt between Q1 and Q2 2012 filings suggesting a large divestiture/deconsolidation with retroactively restated prior-period comparatives
Recurring 10-K/A amendments (2004, 2007, 2009, 2011, 2012) and multiple SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD comment-letter cycles indicating sustained regulatory scrutiny
